dev_tools: (Use ImGui::SameLine() to keep adding items to the right of the preceding item)

(Using ImGui::BeginGroup()/EndGroup() to layout items. BeginGroup() basically locks the horizontal position. EndGroup() bundles the whole group so that you can use functions such as IsItemHovered() on it.)
Check ImGui->Systems->Catalog for invalid catalog items.
Horizontal scrolling for a window has to be enabled explicitly via the ImGuiWindowFlags_HorizontalScrollbar flag.
ImGui is licensed under the MIT License, see LICENSE for more information.
In this ImGui window, cleared entries are grayed out. If "Show Active Status" is checked, entries will be green when the effect is active (the visual effect is only active within a range).
Remember that in many simple cases, you can use ImGui::SameLine(xxx) to position

The logging API redirects all text output so you can easily capture the content of a window or a block. Tree nodes can be automatically expanded. You can also call ImGui::LogText() to output directly to the log without a visual output.
When enabled, ImGui windows can be interacted with by mouse even when input is disabled with Ctrl+Tab
You can use ImGui::GetMouseDragDelta(0) to query for the dragged amount on any widget.
Your application can render a different mouse cursor based on what ImGui::GetMouseCursor() returns. You can also set io.MouseDrawCursor to ask ImGui to render the cursor for you in software.
